The case (upper / lower) may or may not be specified within the `dim_asset_metadata` table, as this column is raw and not transformed, coming directly from the provider APIs. However, in the `ez_` views, it will be lowercase by default for all EVMs.

BLOCK_TIMESTAMP.name: BLOCK_TIMESTAMP
BLOCK_TIMESTAMP.comment: The date and time at which the block was produced.

VERSION.name: VERSION
VERSION.comment: The version also know as the height of the transactions that have been executed on the Aptos blockchain. The first transaction in the blockchain has a version of 0. The version number is incremented by 1 for each transaction that is executed on the blockchain.

TX_HASH.name: TX_HASH

SUCCESS.name: SUCCESS

TX_TYPE.name: TX_TYPE

SENDER.name: SENDER
SENDER.comment: Sender is the address of the originator account for a transaction. A transaction must be signed by the originator.

SIGNATURE.name: SIGNATURE
SIGNATURE.comment: A signature is the result of hashing the signing message with the client's private key. By default Movement uses the Ed25519 scheme to generate the signature of the raw transaction.

PAYLOAD.name: PAYLOAD

PAYLOAD_FUNCTION.name: PAYLOAD_FUNCTION
PAYLOAD_FUNCTION.comment: The function that is being called in the transaction payload.

EVENTS.name: EVENTS

metadata.name: FACT_BLOCKS
metadata.comment:
This table contains "block" level data for the Movement blockchain. This table can be used to analyze trends at a block level, for example total transactions over time.
"The Movement blockchain doesn't have an explicit notion of a block — it only uses blocks for batching and executing transactions.
A transaction at height 0 is the first transaction (genesis transaction), and a transaction at height 100 is the 101st transaction in the transaction store."

BLOCK_NUMBER.name: BLOCK_NUMBER
BLOCK_NUMBER.comment: Also known as block height. The block number, which indicates the length of the blockchain, increases after the addition of each new block.

BLOCK_TIMESTAMP.name: BLOCK_TIMESTAMP

BLOCK_HASH.name: BLOCK_HASH
BLOCK_HASH.comment: The hash of the block header for a given block.

FIRST_VERSION.name: FIRST_VERSION
FIRST_VERSION.comment: The version number of the first transaction in the block.

LAST_VERSION.name: LAST_VERSION
LAST_VERSION.comment: The version number of the last transaction in the block.

TX_COUNT.name: TX_COUNT
TX_COUNT.comment: The count of transactions in this block.

FACT_BLOCKS_ID.name: FACT_BLOCKS_ID
FACT_BLOCKS_ID.comment: The unique identifier for each row in the table.

INSERTED_TIMESTAMP.name: INSERTED_TIMESTAMP
INSERTED_TIMESTAMP.comment: The utc timestamp at which the row was inserted into the table.

MODIFIED_TIMESTAMP.name: MODIFIED_TIMESTAMP

metadata.name: EZ_PRICES_HOURLY
metadata.comment: A convenience table for determining token prices by address and blockchain, and native asset prices by symbol and blockchain. This data set is highly curated and contains metadata for one price per hour per unique asset and blockchain.

HOUR.name: HOUR
HOUR.comment: Hour that the price was recorded at.

TOKEN_ADDRESS.name: TOKEN_ADDRESS
TOKEN_ADDRESS.comment: The specific address representing the asset on a specific platform. This will be NULL if referring to a native asset. The case (upper / lower) may or may not be specified within the `dim_asset_metadata` table, as this column is raw and not transformed, coming directly from the provider APIs. However, in the `ez_` views, it will be lowercase by default for all EVMs.

DECIMALS.name: DECIMALS
DECIMALS.comment: The number of decimals for the asset. May be NULL.

PRICE.name: PRICE
PRICE.comment: Closing price of the recorded hour in USD.

BLOCKCHAIN.name: BLOCKCHAIN
BLOCKCHAIN.comment: The Blockchain, Network, or Platform for this asset.

IS_NATIVE.name: IS_NATIVE
IS_NATIVE.comment: A flag indicating assets native to the respective blockchain.

IS_IMPUTED.name: IS_IMPUTED
IS_IMPUTED.comment: A flag indicating if the price was imputed, or derived, from the last arriving record. This is generally used for tokens with low-liquidity or inconsistent reporting.

IS_DEPRECATED.name: IS_DEPRECATED
IS_DEPRECATED.comment: A flag indicating if the asset is deprecated or no longer supported by the provider.

INSERTED_TIMESTAMP.name: INSERTED_TIMESTAMP
INSERTED_TIMESTAMP.comment: The utc timestamp at which the row was inserted into the table.

MODIFIED_TIMESTAMP.name: MODIFIED_TIMESTAMP
MODIFIED_TIMESTAMP.comment: The utc timestamp at which the row was last modified.

EZ_PRICES_HOURLY_ID.comment: The unique identifier for each row in the table.

metadata.name: FACT_EVENTS
metadata.comment: This table contains the flattened events from the transaction. Each event will have a unique event index within a transaction.

BLOCK_NUMBER.name: BLOCK_NUMBER
BLOCK_NUMBER.comment: Also known as block height. The block number, which indicates the length of the blockchain, increases after the addition of each new block.

BLOCK_TIMESTAMP.name: BLOCK_TIMESTAMP
BLOCK_TIMESTAMP.comment: The date and time at which the block was produced.

TX_HASH.name: TX_HASH
TX_HASH.comment: Transaction hash is a unique 66-character identifier that is generated when a transaction is executed.

VERSION.name: VERSION
VERSION.comment: The version also know as the height of the transactions that have been executed on the Aptos blockchain. The first transaction in the blockchain has a version of 0. The version number is incremented by 1 for each transaction that is executed on the blockchain.

SUCCESS.name: SUCCESS
SUCCESS.comment: The boolean value indicating whether the transaction was successful or not.

TX_TYPE.name: TX_TYPE
TX_TYPE.comment: The type of the transaction. Values will be one of "block_metadata_transaction","state_checkpoint_transaction","user_transaction".

SENDER.name: SENDER
SENDER.comment: Sender is the address of the originator account for a transaction. A transaction must be signed by the originator.

PAYLOAD_FUNCTION.name: PAYLOAD_FUNCTION
PAYLOAD_FUNCTION.comment: The function that is being called in the transaction payload.

EVENT_INDEX.name: EVENT_INDEX
EVENT_INDEX.comment: Unique identifier for the event. This is a monotonically increasing integer that is incremented for each event. This is useful for determining the order of changes.

EVENT_TYPE.name: EVENT_TYPE
EVENT_TYPE.comment: The full three-part descriptive type from event. The event type consists of the event_address :: event_module :: event_resource.

EVENT_ADDRESS.name: EVENT_ADDRESS
EVENT_ADDRESS.comment: The first segment of the event type

EVENT_MODULE.name: EVENT_MODULE
EVENT_MODULE.comment: The second segment of the event type

EVENT_RESOURCE.name: EVENT_RESOURCE
EVENT_RESOURCE.comment: The third segment of the event type

ACCOUNT_ADDRESS.name: ACCOUNT_ADDRESS
ACCOUNT_ADDRESS.comment: The top level address for this event.

CREATION_NUMBER.name: CREATION_NUMBER
CREATION_NUMBER.comment: Ceation number corresponding to the event stream originating from the given account.

SEQUENCE_NUMBER.name: SEQUENCE_NUMBER
SEQUENCE_NUMBER.comment: The sequence number for an account indicates the number of transactions that have been submitted and committed on chain from that account. It is incremented every time a transaction sent from that account is executed or aborted and stored in the blockchain.

FACT_EVENTS_ID.name: FACT_EVENTS_ID
FACT_EVENTS_ID.comment: The unique identifier for each row in the table.

INSERTED_TIMESTAMP.name: INSERTED_TIMESTAMP
INSERTED_TIMESTAMP.comment: The utc timestamp at which the row was inserted into the table.

MODIFIED_TIMESTAMP.name: MODIFIED_TIMESTAMP
MODIFIED_TIMESTAMP.comment: The utc timestamp at which the row was last modified.

metadata.name: FACT_TRANSACTIONS
metadata.comment:
This table contains transaction level data for the Movement blockchain. Each transaction will have a unique transaction hash and version.
For more information see [docs.movementlabs.xyz docs] Each transaction will have a unique transaction hash

BLOCK_NUMBER.name: BLOCK_NUMBER
BLOCK_NUMBER.comment: Also known as block height. The block number, which indicates the length of the blockchain, increases after the addition of each new block.

BLOCK_TIMESTAMP.name: BLOCK_TIMESTAMP
BLOCK_TIMESTAMP.comment: The date and time at which the block was produced.

VERSION.name: VERSION
VERSION.comment: The version also know as the height of the transactions that have been executed on the Aptos blockchain. The first transaction in the blockchain has a version of 0. The version number is incremented by 1 for each transaction that is executed on the blockchain.

TX_HASH.name: TX_HASH
TX_HASH.comment: Transaction hash is a unique 66-character identifier that is generated when a transaction is executed.

SUCCESS.name: SUCCESS
SUCCESS.comment: The boolean value indicating whether the transaction was successful or not.

TX_TYPE.name: TX_TYPE
TX_TYPE.comment: The type of the transaction. Values will be one of "block_metadata_transaction","state_checkpoint_transaction","user_transaction".

SENDER.name: SENDER
SENDER.comment: Sender is the address of the originator account for a transaction. A transaction must be signed by the originator.

SIGNATURE.name: SIGNATURE
SIGNATURE.comment: A signature is the result of hashing the signing message with the client's private key. By default Movement uses the Ed25519 scheme to generate the signature of the raw transaction.

PAYLOAD.name: PAYLOAD
PAYLOAD.comment: The data that is being carried by a transaction.

PAYLOAD_FUNCTION.name: PAYLOAD_FUNCTION
PAYLOAD_FUNCTION.comment: The function that is being called in the transaction payload.

CHANGES.name: CHANGES
CHANGES.comment: The changes that the transaction executed.

EVENTS.name: EVENTS
EVENTS.comment: The events that the transaction executed.

GAS_UNIT_PRICE.name: GAS_UNIT_PRICE
GAS_UNIT_PRICE.comment: The cost per unit of gas, determining the transaction fee paid by the sender for each unit of computational resource consumed

GAS_USED.name: GAS_USED
GAS_USED.comment: The amount of gas used for the transaction

MAX_GAS_AMOUNT.name: MAX_GAS_AMOUNT
MAX_GAS_AMOUNT.comment: The maximum amount of gas allocated for the execution of a transaction

EXPIRATION_TIMESTAMP_SECS.name: EXPIRATION_TIMESTAMP_SECS
EXPIRATION_TIMESTAMP_SECS.comment: The time at which the transaction ceases to valid.

VM_STATUS.name: VM_STATUS
VM_STATUS.comment: For failed transactions, this fields provides context to why the transaction failed. For successful transactions, this field will be set to `Executed successfully`.

ACCUMULATOR_ROOT_HASH.name: ACCUMULATOR_ROOT_HASH
ACCUMULATOR_ROOT_HASH.comment: The root hash of a Merkle accumulator.

EVENT_ROOT_HASH.name: EVENT_ROOT_HASH
EVENT_ROOT_HASH.comment: The root hash for the event.

FACT_TRANSACTIONS_ID.name: FACT_TRANSACTIONS_ID
FACT_TRANSACTIONS_ID.comment: The unique identifier for each row in the table.

INSERTED_TIMESTAMP.name: INSERTED_TIMESTAMP
INSERTED_TIMESTAMP.comment: The utc timestamp at which the row was inserted into the table.

MODIFIED_TIMESTAMP.name: MODIFIED_TIMESTAMP
MODIFIED_TIMESTAMP.comment: The utc timestamp at which the row was last modified.

metadata.name: DIM_ASSET_METADATA
metadata.comment: A comprehensive dimensional table holding asset metadata and other relevant details pertaining to each id, from multiple providers. This data set includes raw, non-transformed data coming directly from the provider APIs and rows are not intended to be unique. As a result, there may be data quality issues persisting in the APIs that flow through to this dimensional model. If you are interested in using a curated data set instead, please utilize ez_asset_metadata.

metadata.name: EZ_DECODED_EVENT_LOGS
metadata.comment:
For information on how to submit a contract for decoding, as well as how ABIs are sourced, please visit [here](https://science.flipsidecrypto.xyz/abi-requestor/).
      This model contains decoded event logs for contracts that we have an ABI for. Please note, this table does not include all event logs, only those that we have an ABI for.
      The `decoded_log` column is the easiest place to query decoded data. It is a JSON object, where the keys are the names of the event parameters, and the values are the values of the event parameters.
      You can select from this column using the following sample format, `decoded_log:from::string` or more generally, `decoded_log:<event_param>::datatype`. See below for a full sample query.
      The `full_decoded_logs` column contains the same information, as well as additional fields such as the datatype of the decoded data. You may need to laterally flatten this column to query the data.
            
      Sample query for USDC Transfer events:
      
      ```sql
      select 
      tx_hash,
      block_number,
      contract_address,
      decoded_log:from::string as from_address,
      decoded_log:to::string as to_address,
      decoded_log:value::integer as value
      from ethereum.core.fact_decoded_event_logs
      where contract_address = lower('0xA0b86991c6218b36c1d19D4a2e9Eb0cE3606eB48')
      and block_number between 16400000 and 16405000
      and event_name = 'Transfer'
      limit 50```

BLOCK_NUMBER.comment: Also known as block height. The block number, which indicates the length of the blockchain, increases after the addition of each new block.

BLOCK_TIMESTAMP.name: BLOCK_TIMESTAMP
BLOCK_TIMESTAMP.comment: The date and time at which the block was produced.

TX_HASH.name: TX_HASH
TX_HASH.comment: Transaction hash is a unique 66-character identifier that is generated when a transaction is executed.

TX_POSITION.name: TX_POSITION
TX_POSITION.comment: The position of the transaction within the block.

CONTRACT_ADDRESS.name: CONTRACT_ADDRESS
CONTRACT_ADDRESS.comment: The address of the contract that the event log was emitted from.

TOPIC_0.comment: The first topic of the event, which is a unique identifier for the event.

TOPIC_1.comment: The second topic of the event, if applicable.

TOPIC_2.comment: The third topic of the event, if applicable.

TOPIC_3.comment: The fourth topic of the event, if applicable.

EVENT_REMOVED.comment: Whether the event has been removed from the transaction.
